jobs.title: Senior Manager, Global Merchandising (icebreaker)
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF2
jobs.description:
Icebreaker is looking for an exceptional Sr Manager, Global Product Merchandising to join our team based in Stabio, Switzerland.

Driven by the belief that nature has the answers, we provide natural performance alternatives to synthetic-based apparel, to create a healthier and more sustainable future for our species and the planet. We're passionate about connecting people to natural alternatives.

Let’s Talk about the Role

As Senior Manager, Global Product Merchandising , you will drive product excellence by leading and unifying the Merchandising Business Analysis and Product Merchandising teams in the execution of the icebreaker product line through the GTM process from concept to finished goods ensuring alignment with the brand strategy, global business needs, sourcing capabilities and quality standards.

This role leads the consumer-centric planning, briefing, pricing and GTM execution of industry leading product to meet critical business and timeline expectations, collaborating cross functionally with a multi-season time horizon perspective.

A key leader in the product creation team you will support and enable the Global Product Director’s strategic vision and inspire your team, unlocking their potential to turn product concepts into reality to drive business growth.

How You Will Make a Difference

Leadership & Oversight :

Provide visionary leadership for the Merchandising Business Analysis and Product Merchandising functions. Develop and implement guidelines and processes to ensure the successful delivery of products that drive the brand’s strategic and commercial objectives. Support the personal development of the Product Merchandising associates, through coaching and by providing opportunities for growth with a focus on product marketplace knowledge & category expertise.

End-to-End Product Excellence :

Oversee the complete product lifecycle, translating consumer and marketplace insights through clear briefs and guiding execution with an emphasis on profitability and efficiency to ensure products meet the strategic business targets. Maintain a long-range outlook, proactively identifying new commercial opportunities and managing product lifecycles to ensure products are continually upgraded, enhanced and the best they can be, aligned with Icebreaker’s performance, sustainability, and quality standards.

Cross-Functional Collaboration :

Serve as the operational interface between internal departments (Design, Product Development, Supply Planning, Marketing) and external stakeholders (Regional Merchandising and Sales teams). Establish strong working relationships, facilitate transparent communication and collaboration to ensure alignment on product strategies, timelines, forecasting and positioning.

Process Optimisation & Best Practices :

Lead the implementation of the global product strategy ensuring cross-functional seasonal alignment in executing the end-to-end GTM process and ensuring that on-time completion of all critical deadlines and required inputs/outputs is achieved. Prepare compelling seasonal product assortment plans and pricing architectures through the formulation of product briefs that meet revenue and margin targets. Be the consumer expert and (in partnership with regional merchandising) proactively gather marketplace insights to inform, continuously improve and validate product direction.

Stakeholder Management :

Develop and maintain strong relationships with the Regional Merchandising teams. Take ownership of the introduction of new icebreaker products, ensuring clear and concise direction to design and development teams through the briefing and the product creation process. Deliver inspiring product presentations to support the GTM process and empower regional stakeholders (merchandising, sales and marketing teams) with the seasonal product strategy and new product features and benefits.

Reporting & Strategic Input :

Report directly to the Global Product Director, providing regular updates on the team’s progress against GTM timelines, process improvements, and cross-functional initiatives. Contribute to high-level strategic planning and decision-making to deliver the brand’s tactical and commercial objectives.

Skills for Success

Vision:

A forward-thinking mindset to anticipate consumer and market trends in sustainable outdoor performance apparel which inform merchandising strategies and concepts.

Values :

A commitment to sustainability, an innovative, consumer-centric approach to product creation, detail oriented with an appreciation of high-quality and aesthetic standards in execution.

Expertise :

In-depth understanding of data analysis, line planning, briefing, costing and forecasting processes. Deep understanding of PLM systems and spreadsheet creation/management (Microsoft Excel).

Communication :

Excellent interpersonal and presentation skills with the ability to influence and facilitate effective collaboration and negotiation between internal and external stakeholders ensuring alignment and transparency across all levels.

Agility : Expertise in managing complex, multi-phase projects with a keen understanding of go to market strategy, agile methodologies, and process optimization. Flexibility to work and travel in an international context across regions and associated time zones, at times requiring meeting attendance early mornings or evenings.

Education & Experience

A minimum of 10 years of progressive experience in product development and merchandising roles within the apparel industry, with at least 5 years in a management position. Experience in the outdoor performance or sustainable apparel sector is highly preferred.

High level of computer literacy and an affinity for technology; proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel & PowerPoint) with a deep understanding Product Line Management (Enovia, Flex) solutions.

What’s in it For You

We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we’re the total package.

- A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do

- An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together

- Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization

- On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives

- A discount card with 50% on all VF brands

- Break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks

Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity

As a purpose-led, performance driven company, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day.

As an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer, VF is committed to support disadvantaged groups whilst providing equal opportunities for both VF associates and applicants alike.

If you like what you have read and want to join our team then we would like to hear from you!

R-20260507-0015

jobs.addressLocality: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF2

jobs.title: Customs Specialist – EMEA
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description:
VF Corporation is looking for an exceptional Customs Specialist – EMEA to join our team based in Stabio, Switzerland.

Let’s talk about the role!

As a Customs Specialist EMEA, you will play a critical role in ensuring our import and export operations meet all legal and business requirements. You’ll be part of a high-performing team that supports compliance across multiple regions, with a strong focus on:

- Customs Compliance: Ensure adherence to customs regulations for shipments from Asia Pacific and Latin America to the EU and UK.

- Trade Expertise: Apply your customs knowledge to support smooth operations. In this role, the classification of our products will be the main key element of the day-to-day workload.

- Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with internal departments (e.g., product teams, logistics, master data) and external stakeholders (e.g., consultant, customs authorities, brokers, carriers) to document & drive the classification process.

- Process Improvement: Take part in partnering with external parties to fine-tune the classification process and tools used. This includes an AI driven engine that supports the classification process in 4 jurisdictions across Europe and is linked to a global roll-out on classification across the globe (US & Asia)

Skills for Success:

- Bachelor’s degree in economics, Law, Supply Chain, or a related field (or equivalent experience).

- A few years of progressive experience in customs or trade compliance and more specifically a strong connection to the classification process of products according to the EU tariff scheme.

- Strong understanding of customs legislation and operational processes.

- Excellent analytical and organizational skills, with the ability to manage complex data sets.

- Fluent in English (written and verbal); additional languages are a plus.

- Proficient in Microsoft Office and other relevant systems, open to learn & work with the AI driven engine (Trade Classifier) used in today’s processes.

- Occasional travel within Europe is required to support audits, team collaboration, and engagement with customs authorities and external partners

About VF

VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at vfc.com

What’s in it For You

We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we’re the total package.

- A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do

- An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together

- Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization

- On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives

- A discount card with 50% on all VF brands

- Break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks

Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity

As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day.

If you like what you have read and want to join our team then we would like to hear from you!

R-20260507-0009

descriptionByLocale.it:
VF Corporation is looking for an exceptional Customs Specialist – EMEA to join our team based in Stabio, Switzerland.

Let’s talk about the role!

As a Customs Specialist EMEA, you will play a critical role in ensuring our import and export operations meet all legal and business requirements. You’ll be part of a high-performing team that supports compliance across multiple regions, with a strong focus on:

- Customs Compliance: Ensure adherence to customs regulations for shipments from Asia Pacific and Latin America to the EU and UK.

- Trade Expertise: Apply your customs knowledge to support smooth operations. In this role, the classification of our products will be the main key element of the day-to-day workload.

- Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with internal departments (e.g., product teams, logistics, master data) and external stakeholders (e.g., consultant, customs authorities, brokers, carriers) to document & drive the classification process.

- Process Improvement: Take part in partnering with external parties to fine-tune the classification process and tools used. This includes an AI driven engine that supports the classification process in 4 jurisdictions across Europe and is linked to a global roll-out on classification across the globe (US & Asia)

Skills for Success:

- Bachelor’s degree in economics, Law, Supply Chain, or a related field (or equivalent experience).

- A few years of progressive experience in customs or trade compliance and more specifically a strong connection to the classification process of products according to the EU tariff scheme.

- Strong understanding of customs legislation and operational processes.

- Excellent analytical and organizational skills, with the ability to manage complex data sets.

- Fluent in English (written and verbal); additional languages are a plus.

- Proficient in Microsoft Office and other relevant systems, open to learn & work with the AI driven engine (Trade Classifier) used in today’s processes.

- Occasional travel within Europe is required to support audits, team collaboration, and engagement with customs authorities and external partners

About VF

VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at vfc.com

What’s in it For You

We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we’re the total package.

- A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do

- An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together

- Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization

- On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives

- A discount card with 50% on all VF brands

- Break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks

Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity

As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day.

If you like what you have read and want to join our team then we would like to hear from you!

R-20260507-0009

jobs.title: Regulatory Compliance Counsel
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description:
VF Corporation is looking for an exceptional Regulatory Compliance Counsel to join our Legal team based in Stabio, Switzerland.

Let’s talk about the role!

As Regulatory Compliance Counsel, you will play a key role in advancing VF’s global Ethics and Compliance Program. You will partner with Ethics & Compliance, Legal, Finance, HR, and business leaders worldwide. The Regulatory Compliance Counsel will lead VF’s European Antitrust Compliance Program and act as the regional leader in EMEA and APAC for VF’s broader global Ethics & Compliance program. This role reports to the Vice President, Ethics & Compliance.

- Collaborate with the global Ethics & Compliance team, the EMEA Legal team and business leaders to lead the risk-based Antitrust Compliance Program in Europe. This may include site visits, live and online training creation and delivery, policy and communications plan enhancements, and collaboration with internal audit, etc. Identify potential antitrust issues arising in wholesale operations and help ensure VF’s practices remain compliant.

- Stay abreast of pertinent legal developments relating to antitrust within the Region, and translate key developments into practical, risk-based guidance for VF stakeholders (in particular those supporting wholesale activities).

- Support the conceptualization, establishment, and upkeep of compliance tools and protocols that help VF prevent, detect, and address antitrust risk in connection with wholesale activities.

- Enhance and implement the global Environmental and Social Responsibility (E&SR) reporting compliance program, focused on material E&SR reporting regulations, such as the EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD) and non-EU ISSB-aligned disclosure regulations.

- Proactively champion the Ethics and Compliance Program and advise on how to adapt global initiatives within EMEA and APAC.

- Oversee, direct, develop, or ensure delivery of training and awareness initiatives to relevant employees.

- Support regional aspects of risk assessments and related ongoing monitoring activities in collaboration with the global Ethics & Compliance team.

- Engage with regional leadership to ensure understanding, support and buy in for the Ethics & Compliance program, antitrust compliance program and E&SR regulated reporting compliance program; convey complex information and discern when and how to engage leaders to resolve issues; report information on a regular basis to keep management informed of compliance efforts and proactively raise issues and potential gaps.

- Serve as liaison between Ethics and Compliance and business stakeholders to streamline compliance processes, protect the best interest of VF globally, and elevate issues to VF’s global Ethics and Compliance team.

- Provide general advice on latest regulatory and compliance developments in EMEA and APAC, including in the areas of antitrust, and environmental, social and governance regulations, including, for example, the EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive and Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive.

Skills for Success

The ideal candidate will possess the following qualifications and skills:

- Educational background: US or European Law Degree.

- Strong Regulatory compliance experience, ideally related to anti-corruption, sanctions, antitrust and/or environmental, or governance regulations. Strong understanding of antitrust law within the region.

- Previous successful business or legal experience in a relevant field that demonstrates a track record of success and an ability to develop efficient and effective policies, systems and processes.

- Substantive regulatory compliance experience with a corporate legal department is a plus.

- Strong collaboration skills and ability to provide risk-based advice in a global corporate environment.

- Keen understanding of the wholesale ecosystem, including the nuances of both physical and online distribution channels, to effectively identify and manage antitrust risk in relevant commercial scenarios.

- Availability to travel up to 25%.

- Excellent written and oral communication skills in English.

About VF

VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at vfc.com

What’s in it For You

We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we’re the total package.

- A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do

- An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together

- Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization

- On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives

- A discount card with 50% on all VF brands

- Break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks

Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity

As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day.

If you like what you have read and want to join our team then we would like to hear from you!

R-20260429-0005

R-20260429-0005
descriptionByLocale.fr:
VF Corporation is looking for an exceptional Regulatory Compliance Counsel to join our Legal team based in Stabio, Switzerland.

Let’s talk about the role!

As Regulatory Compliance Counsel, you will play a key role in advancing VF’s global Ethics and Compliance Program. You will partner with Ethics & Compliance, Legal, Finance, HR, and business leaders worldwide. The Regulatory Compliance Counsel will lead VF’s European Antitrust Compliance Program and act as the regional leader in EMEA and APAC for VF’s broader global Ethics & Compliance program. This role reports to the Vice President, Ethics & Compliance.

- Collaborate with the global Ethics & Compliance team, the EMEA Legal team and business leaders to lead the risk-based Antitrust Compliance Program in Europe. This may include site visits, live and online training creation and delivery, policy and communications plan enhancements, and collaboration with internal audit, etc. Identify potential antitrust issues arising in wholesale operations and help ensure VF’s practices remain compliant.

- Stay abreast of pertinent legal developments relating to antitrust within the Region, and translate key developments into practical, risk-based guidance for VF stakeholders (in particular those supporting wholesale activities).

- Support the conceptualization, establishment, and upkeep of compliance tools and protocols that help VF prevent, detect, and address antitrust risk in connection with wholesale activities.

- Enhance and implement the global Environmental and Social Responsibility (E&SR) reporting compliance program, focused on material E&SR reporting regulations, such as the EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD) and non-EU ISSB-aligned disclosure regulations.

- Proactively champion the Ethics and Compliance Program and advise on how to adapt global initiatives within EMEA and APAC.

- Oversee, direct, develop, or ensure delivery of training and awareness initiatives to relevant employees.

- Support regional aspects of risk assessments and related ongoing monitoring activities in collaboration with the global Ethics & Compliance team.

- Engage with regional leadership to ensure understanding, support and buy in for the Ethics & Compliance program, antitrust compliance program and E&SR regulated reporting compliance program; convey complex information and discern when and how to engage leaders to resolve issues; report information on a regular basis to keep management informed of compliance efforts and proactively raise issues and potential gaps.

- Serve as liaison between Ethics and Compliance and business stakeholders to streamline compliance processes, protect the best interest of VF globally, and elevate issues to VF’s global Ethics and Compliance team.

- Provide general advice on latest regulatory and compliance developments in EMEA and APAC, including in the areas of antitrust, and environmental, social and governance regulations, including, for example, the EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive and Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive.

Skills for Success

The ideal candidate will possess the following qualifications and skills:

- Educational background: US or European Law Degree.

- Strong Regulatory compliance experience, ideally related to anti-corruption, sanctions, antitrust and/or environmental, or governance regulations. Strong understanding of antitrust law within the region.

- Previous successful business or legal experience in a relevant field that demonstrates a track record of success and an ability to develop efficient and effective policies, systems and processes.

- Substantive regulatory compliance experience with a corporate legal department is a plus.

- Strong collaboration skills and ability to provide risk-based advice in a global corporate environment.

- Keen understanding of the wholesale ecosystem, including the nuances of both physical and online distribution channels, to effectively identify and manage antitrust risk in relevant commercial scenarios.

- Availability to travel up to 25%.

- Excellent written and oral communication skills in English.

About VF

VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at vfc.com

What’s in it For You

We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we’re the total package.

- A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do

- An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together

- Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization

- On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives

- A discount card with 50% on all VF brands

- Break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks

Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity

As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day.

jobs.title: Senior Outerwear Designer, Global Apparel (Timberland)
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description:
Timberland is looking for an exceptional Senior Outerwear Designer, Global Apparel to join our Timberland team based in Stabio, Switzerland.

Let’s Talk about the Role

We’re looking for a Senior Outerwear Designer to design for men’s and women’s outerwear .

Your mission? Create products that stand out and perform, blending style and function—always true to Timberland’s DNA.

We design with purpose. Our Outerwear is crafted for the elements—weather-ready and a rugged style.

Timberland is a force of nature, creating products that are bold, resilient, and unapologetically authentic.

From our Iconic Yellow Boot to Outerwear that blends our heritage and innovation, we believe in simpler, stronger craft for people with an unbreakable spirit. If you thrive on creativity, collaboration, and making a statement, this is your stage.

How You Will Make a Difference

-
Own the design process from concept to final product.

-
Understand our consumer through market research.

-
Push boundaries with fresh, authentic ideas.

-
Explore your craft with both traditional and modern tools.

-
Collaborate with Development, Merchandising, and Product Specialists.

-
Visit vendors to optimize your products.

-
Champion intent and purpose in every design.

-
Mentor and foster creativity.

Skills for Success

-
8+ years of experience in outerwear design.

-
Strong knowledge of construction, materials, and fit.

-
A portfolio that shows bold thinking and refined execution.

-
Proficiency in Adobe Illustrator; AI and 3D skills are a plus.

-
Strong presentation skills.

jobs.title: Senior Manager, Product Sustainability & Circularity
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description:
VF Corporation is looking for a Senior Manager, Product Sustainability & Circularity to join our Global Sustainability team based in Stabio, Switzerland.

Let’s talk about the role!

VF Corporation is seeking a Senior Manager, Product Sustainability & Circularity to join its Global Sustainability team. This role works across VF and its portfolio of brands to advance circularity, product sustainability, and regulatory readiness, with a strong focus on EU requirements.

You will partner closely with design, development, and brand teams to embed sustainability into early product decisions, translating global strategy and regulatory requirements into clear design guidance and scalable execution. The role connects global sustainability strategy with regional and brand‑level delivery, leveraging circularity to drive compliance, innovation, and long‑term value creation.

As part of a small, high‑impact team, you will lead initiatives, manage a team, and engage internal and external stakeholders to prepare VF brands for current and emerging sustainability regulations while contributing to VF’s global sustainability goals.

How You Will Make a Difference

As Senior Manager, Product Sustainability & Circularity, you will be responsible for the following key activities and programs:

Strategy & Leadership

- Define and guide VF’s Circular Product Design strategy, aligned with global sustainability objectives, brand priorities, and emerging regulatory requirements.

- Contribute to VF’s global impact reduction, compliance, and reporting strategies as a member of the Global Sustainability Leadership Team.

- Act as a bridge between global strategy and brand execution, ensuring consistency and delivery across regions.

Circularity & Product Integration

- Partner with cross‑functional VF and Brand teams to integrate circular economy principles across product creation and business operations, including design, development, innovation, marketing, logistics, and retail.

- Drive adoption of more sustainable and circular materials and technologies in collaboration with sustainable materials teams.

- Support VF’s Head of Recommerce as the regional conduit for resale, rental, and recommerce initiatives.

Regulatory Preparedness & Compliance

- Provide strategic direction to VF functions and Brand teams on the sustainability and circularity regulatory landscape, ensuring compliance and progress toward voluntary targets.

- Lead EU Ban on the Destruction of Unsold Goods preparedness, partnering with Brands to develop compliant outflow solutions.

- Lead the development and rollout of Brand Repair programs in EMEA, including collection of required regulatory data.

Data, Reporting & Disclosure

- Identify circularity‑related data needs for mandatory and voluntary disclosures (e.g., CSRD).

- Lead workstreams to improve data quality, access, and governance, in collaboration with Digital Technology, Legal, Government Affairs, Global Sustainability, and Brand teams.

Stakeholder Engagement & Representation

- Partner with EMEA leadership teams to keep sustainability and regulatory readiness central to brand priorities.

- Engage with key EMEA retailers to position VF brands strongly on product sustainability and circularity.

- Represent VF internally and externally at industry associations, conferences, and company forums

Skills for Success

- Strong understanding of the product creation cycle for apparel, footwear, and equipment.

- Strong grounding in environmental policy, and EU governance and public policy.

- An understanding of corporate business processes and the industry operating model.

- Familiarity with key sustainability concepts and frameworks, e.g. circularity, decarbonisation levers, LCA, regenerative agriculture, nature impact.

- People leader with a passion for cultivating great team culture and mission.

- A boundless curiosity for the planet, nature and thriving ecosystems.

- Self-motivation & self-starter skills, proactive, & able to work independently with limited supervision.

- Ability to successfully support and prioritize multiple projects and teams at one time.

- Exposure to sustainability ratings and reporting frameworks including, but not limited to, GHG Protocol, Science Based Targets initiative, Net Zero, TCFD, CDP and CSRD.

- An ease to manoeuvre between different stakeholder groups and an ability to reconcile diverse inputs into an aligned pathway forward.

- Systems thinker with an ability to understand underlying connections and correlations.

- Excellent written and verbal communication and interpersonal skills to effectively work cross-functionally across the organization, and with external consultants.

- Strong communicator and enjoys working with people.

Educational/ Position Requirements:

- Bachelor’s degree in product design, product development, sustainability, CSR, economics, environmental studies, management information systems, engineering, finance, or similar field

- Masters degree preferred.

What’s in it For You

We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we’re the total package.

- A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do

- An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together

- Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization

- On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives

- A discount card with 50% on all VF brands

- Break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks

Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity

As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day.

If you like what you have read and want to join our team then we would like to hear from you!

Let’s talk about the role!

VF Corporation is seeking a Senior Manager, Product Sustainability & Circularity to join its Global Sustainability team. This role works across VF and its portfolio of brands to advance circularity, product sustainability, and regulatory readiness, with a strong focus on EU requirements.

You will partner closely with design, development, and brand teams to embed sustainability into early product decisions, translating global strategy and regulatory requirements into clear design guidance and scalable execution. The role connects global sustainability strategy with regional and brand‑level delivery, leveraging circularity to drive compliance, innovation, and long‑term value creation.

As part of a small, high‑impact team, you will lead initiatives, manage a team, and engage internal and external stakeholders to prepare VF brands for current and emerging sustainability regulations while contributing to VF’s global sustainability goals.

How You Will Make a Difference

As Senior Manager, Product Sustainability & Circularity, you will be responsible for the following key activities and programs:

Strategy & Leadership

- Define and guide VF’s Circular Product Design strategy, aligned with global sustainability objectives, brand priorities, and emerging regulatory requirements.

- Contribute to VF’s global impact reduction, compliance, and reporting strategies as a member of the Global Sustainability Leadership Team.

- Act as a bridge between global strategy and brand execution, ensuring consistency and delivery across regions.

Circularity & Product Integration

- Partner with cross‑functional VF and Brand teams to integrate circular economy principles across product creation and business operations, including design, development, innovation, marketing, logistics, and retail.

- Drive adoption of more sustainable and circular materials and technologies in collaboration with sustainable materials teams.

- Support VF’s Head of Recommerce as the regional conduit for resale, rental, and recommerce initiatives.

Regulatory Preparedness & Compliance

- Provide strategic direction to VF functions and Brand teams on the sustainability and circularity regulatory landscape, ensuring compliance and progress toward voluntary targets.

- Lead EU Ban on the Destruction of Unsold Goods preparedness, partnering with Brands to develop compliant outflow solutions.

- Lead the development and rollout of Brand Repair programs in EMEA, including collection of required regulatory data.

Data, Reporting & Disclosure

- Identify circularity‑related data needs for mandatory and voluntary disclosures (e.g., CSRD).

- Lead workstreams to improve data quality, access, and governance, in collaboration with Digital Technology, Legal, Government Affairs, Global Sustainability, and Brand teams.

Stakeholder Engagement & Representation

- Partner with EMEA leadership teams to keep sustainability and regulatory readiness central to brand priorities.

- Engage with key EMEA retailers to position VF brands strongly on product sustainability and circularity.

- Represent VF internally and externally at industry associations, conferences, and company forums

Skills for Success

- Strong understanding of the product creation cycle for apparel, footwear, and equipment.

- Strong grounding in environmental policy, and EU governance and public policy.

- An understanding of corporate business processes and the industry operating model.

- Familiarity with key sustainability concepts and frameworks, e.g. circularity, decarbonisation levers, LCA, regenerative agriculture, nature impact.

- People leader with a passion for cultivating great team culture and mission.

- A boundless curiosity for the planet, nature and thriving ecosystems.

- Self-motivation & self-starter skills, proactive, & able to work independently with limited supervision.

- Ability to successfully support and prioritize multiple projects and teams at one time.

- Exposure to sustainability ratings and reporting frameworks including, but not limited to, GHG Protocol, Science Based Targets initiative, Net Zero, TCFD, CDP and CSRD.

- An ease to manoeuvre between different stakeholder groups and an ability to reconcile diverse inputs into an aligned pathway forward.

- Systems thinker with an ability to understand underlying connections and correlations.

- Excellent written and verbal communication and interpersonal skills to effectively work cross-functionally across the organization, and with external consultants.

- Strong communicator and enjoys working with people.

Educational/ Position Requirements:

- Bachelor’s degree in product design, product development, sustainability, CSR, economics, environmental studies, management information systems, engineering, finance, or similar field

- Masters degree preferred.

What’s in it For You

We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we’re the total package.

- A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do

- An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together

- Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization

- On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives

- A discount card with 50% on all VF brands

- Break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks

Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity

As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day.

If you like what you have read and want to join our team then we would like to hear from you!

jobs.title: Manager, GTM & Marketplace Strategy (Altra Running EMEA)
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description:
Altra Running is looking for an exceptional Manager, GTM & Marketplace Strategy to join our team based in Stabio, Switzerland .

Let’s Talk about the Role

As Manager, GTM & Marketplace Strategy for Altra Running, you will play a critical role in driving seasonal execution through an integrated Go‑To‑Market process and defining a clear and disciplined Marketplace Strategy across EMEA.

Partnering closely with the General Manager EMEA & APAC and regional cross‑functional leaders, you will plan, execute and assess initiatives that drive revenue growth and profitability while protecting brand equity, marketplace integrity and consumer experience.

You will work in close collaboration with the Global GTM Director based in Denver, ensuring full alignment between regional execution and global GTM strategy, marketplace governance and brand priorities.

Acting as the guardian of GTM milestones and marketplace governance, you will orchestrate preparation, execution and performance tracking across Digital Marketplaces, Wholesale and DTC, delivering key insights and recommended actions to Altra Running stakeholders.

How You Will Make a Difference

You will make an impact through the following key areas:

Marketplace Strategy & Protection

-
Define and drive a clear Marketplace Strategy aligned with Altra Running’s brand positioning and long‑term growth ambitions.

-
Ensure robust marketplace protection, including:

-
Assortment control and brand representation

-
Price architecture, channel discipline and segmentation enforcement

-
Compliance with contractual, legal and financial frameworks

-
Partner closely with Finance and Legal teams to manage marketplace models, contracts, margins and risk exposure.

GTM Process & Operations

-
Partner with E‑commerce, Merchandising, Marketing and Area Management teams to lead seasonal GTM processes, ensuring marketplaces are fully integrated into the GTM calendar.

-
Work closely with the Global GTM Director to align regional GTM plans with global strategy, milestones and priorities.

-
Drive execution of GTM deliverables, including:

-
Marketplace go‑live timing

-
Seasonal assortment drops

-
Channel‑specific GTM priorities

-
Ensure end‑to‑end execution of strategy at every GTM milestone.

Segmentation & Channel Governance

-
Define and enforce clear channel and marketplace segmentation across:

-
DTC

-
Strategic wholesale partners

-
Digital marketplaces

-
Translate segmentation into actionable:

-
Assortment strategies

-
Go‑to‑market priorities

-
Regional and account‑level execution

-
Act as a strategic reference point to prevent channel conflict, brand dilution and price erosion.

Cross‑Channel Performance Tracking & Reporting

-
Track and analyze key seasonal and marketplace KPIs to assess brand performance and execution quality.

-
Identify variances, propose corrective actions and provide strategic guidance to stakeholders.

-
Deliver executive‑level reporting on:

-
Sell‑through

-
Inventory exposure

-
Price integrity

-
Margin and profitability

-
Partner with commercial, product, DTC and planning teams to monitor business performance and drive actions.

Cross‑Functional Leadership

-
Act as a strategic peer and partner to:

-
E‑commerce Managers

-
Merchandising teams

-
Area Managers

-
Head of Marketing

-
Finance and Legal teams

-
Drive alignment, challenge assumptions and foster collaboration across regional and global stakeholders.

Skills for Success

-
University degree or equivalent, preferably in Business, Economics or a related field.

-
Strong experience in GTM, Marketplace management, E‑commerce or Commercial Strategy .

-
Solid financial acumen with the ability to work with KPIs, reporting tools and performance analysis.

-
Highly analytical, strategic and structured mindset.

-
Proven stakeholder management and cross‑functional leadership skills.

-
Excellent communication skills in English; German is a plus .

-
Strong passion for sports and the sporting goods industry.

-
Results‑driven, proactive and ownership‑oriented approach.

What’s in it for you?

Most companies like to say they offer a competitive salary, an amazing bonus/benefit and pension scheme as well as staff discounts (btw we offer 50% on all VF Brands!). We also do this, only quite different. Because it’s not just our products which set us apart from others. It’s our people and we believe they deserve to be nurtured and looked after.

That’s why, on top of the usual benefits, we offer much more:

-
Career ownership, enabling you to build your knowledge and experience across different brands and even different countries.

-
A supportive feedback‑based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do.

-
Tailored training. From a thorough induction to ongoing online and face‑to‑face training, we are committed to helping you grow, both professionally and personally.

-
An inclusive environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together.

-
On‑site gym offering health and well‑being initiatives.

-
Subsidised canteen and break‑out areas offering complimentary hot drinks.

About VF

VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at http://vfc.com .

Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging

As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day.

R-20260420-0019
descriptionByLocale.de:
Altra Running is looking for an exceptional Manager, GTM & Marketplace Strategy to join our team based in Stabio, Switzerland .

Let’s Talk about the Role

As Manager, GTM & Marketplace Strategy for Altra Running, you will play a critical role in driving seasonal execution through an integrated Go‑To‑Market process and defining a clear and disciplined Marketplace Strategy across EMEA.

Partnering closely with the General Manager EMEA & APAC and regional cross‑functional leaders, you will plan, execute and assess initiatives that drive revenue growth and profitability while protecting brand equity, marketplace integrity and consumer experience.

You will work in close collaboration with the Global GTM Director based in Denver, ensuring full alignment between regional execution and global GTM strategy, marketplace governance and brand priorities.

Acting as the guardian of GTM milestones and marketplace governance, you will orchestrate preparation, execution and performance tracking across Digital Marketplaces, Wholesale and DTC, delivering key insights and recommended actions to Altra Running stakeholders.

How You Will Make a Difference

You will make an impact through the following key areas:

Marketplace Strategy & Protection

-
Define and drive a clear Marketplace Strategy aligned with Altra Running’s brand positioning and long‑term growth ambitions.

-
Ensure robust marketplace protection, including:

-
Assortment control and brand representation

-
Price architecture, channel discipline and segmentation enforcement

-
Compliance with contractual, legal and financial frameworks

-
Partner closely with Finance and Legal teams to manage marketplace models, contracts, margins and risk exposure.

GTM Process & Operations

-
Partner with E‑commerce, Merchandising, Marketing and Area Management teams to lead seasonal GTM processes, ensuring marketplaces are fully integrated into the GTM calendar.

-
Work closely with the Global GTM Director to align regional GTM plans with global strategy, milestones and priorities.

-
Drive execution of GTM deliverables, including:

-
Marketplace go‑live timing

-
Seasonal assortment drops

-
Channel‑specific GTM priorities

-
Ensure end‑to‑end execution of strategy at every GTM milestone.

Segmentation & Channel Governance

-
Define and enforce clear channel and marketplace segmentation across:

-
DTC

-
Strategic wholesale partners

-
Digital marketplaces

-
Translate segmentation into actionable:

-
Assortment strategies

-
Go‑to‑market priorities

-
Regional and account‑level execution

-
Act as a strategic reference point to prevent channel conflict, brand dilution and price erosion.

Cross‑Channel Performance Tracking & Reporting

-
Track and analyze key seasonal and marketplace KPIs to assess brand performance and execution quality.

-
Identify variances, propose corrective actions and provide strategic guidance to stakeholders.

-
Deliver executive‑level reporting on:

-
Sell‑through

-
Inventory exposure

-
Price integrity

-
Margin and profitability

-
Partner with commercial, product, DTC and planning teams to monitor business performance and drive actions.

Cross‑Functional Leadership

-
Act as a strategic peer and partner to:

-
E‑commerce Managers

-
Merchandising teams

-
Area Managers

-
Head of Marketing

-
Finance and Legal teams

-
Drive alignment, challenge assumptions and foster collaboration across regional and global stakeholders.

Skills for Success

-
University degree or equivalent, preferably in Business, Economics or a related field.

-
Strong experience in GTM, Marketplace management, E‑commerce or Commercial Strategy .

-
Solid financial acumen with the ability to work with KPIs, reporting tools and performance analysis.

-
Highly analytical, strategic and structured mindset.

-
Proven stakeholder management and cross‑functional leadership skills.

-
Excellent communication skills in English; German is a plus .

-
Strong passion for sports and the sporting goods industry.

-
Results‑driven, proactive and ownership‑oriented approach.

What’s in it for you?

Most companies like to say they offer a competitive salary, an amazing bonus/benefit and pension scheme as well as staff discounts (btw we offer 50% on all VF Brands!). We also do this, only quite different. Because it’s not just our products which set us apart from others. It’s our people and we believe they deserve to be nurtured and looked after.

That’s why, on top of the usual benefits, we offer much more:

-
Career ownership, enabling you to build your knowledge and experience across different brands and even different countries.

-
A supportive feedback‑based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do.

-
Tailored training. From a thorough induction to ongoing online and face‑to‑face training, we are committed to helping you grow, both professionally and personally.

-
An inclusive environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together.

-
On‑site gym offering health and well‑being initiatives.

-
Subsidised canteen and break‑out areas offering complimentary hot drinks.

About VF

VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at http://vfc.com .

Free to Be, Inclusion & Belonging

As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day.

jobs.title: Inventory Controller (Temporary)
jobs.location: EMEA · CHE · Stabio · VF Campus VF1
jobs.description:
VF Corporation is looking for an exceptional Inventory Controller (temporary 9 months) to join our Sales Audit and Inventory Control team based in Stabio Switzerland .

Let’s talk about the role!

The role is designed to provide strong operational support to the Sales Audit and Inventory Control team with specific focus on Concession, Consignment and Digital accounts Inventory reconciliation.

The successful candidate will be expected to:

-
Support the ongoing maintenance and update of all weekly inventory recon analysis

-
Assist in the development, enhancement, and testing of additional tools aimed at improving inventory reconciliation processes

-
Provide dedicated support to inventory reconciliation activities with a specific focus on the digital customer Zalando, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and timely issue resolution

Skills for Success:

The ideal candidate will possess the following qualifications and skills:

-
Educational Background: master’s degree in Finance, Accounting or Economics

-
Professional Experience: At least a few years of in the Finance department of a company, preferably in multinational environment under US GAAP, and/or Big Four Language

-
Proficiency: Fluency in English is essential (written and spoken)

-
Strong analytical and organizational skills

-
Excellent Excel skills

-
Knowledge of SAP Platform

-
Knowledge of IBM Cognos Business Intelligence

-
Teamwork and Diversity: Enthusiastic about working and socializing in a diverse, inclusive, and multicultural environment.

-
Personal Attributes: Proactive, meticulous, and reliable with a positive mindset and a keen desire to learn and grow professionally.

About VF

VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at vfc.com

What’s in it For You:

We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we’re the total package.

-
A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do

-
An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together.

-
Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization

-
On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives

-
50% discount on all VF brands

-
Break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks.

This post is not eligible for relocation support.

Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity

As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day.

If you like what you have read and want to join our team, then we would like to hear from you.
